items.title: Arrive El Dorado Airport
items.detail: Terminal 1 international arrivals. Uber or taxi to La Candelaria ~COP 25,000 ($6), 40 min depending on traffic.

items.title: Check into hotel in La Candelaria
items.detail: Historic quarter with colonial architecture, street art, and cafés. Walking distance to major sights.

items.title: Lunch at Andrés Carne de Res D.C.
items.detail: Iconic Colombian restaurant — grilled meats, arepas, and live atmosphere. Bogotá branch in Zona T. Budget ~COP 50,000 ($12).

items.title: Museo del Oro (Gold Museum)
items.detail: 55,000 gold pieces from pre-Columbian civilizations. One of South America's most important museums. Entry ~COP 5,000 ($1.20). Free on Sundays.

items.title: Museo Botero & La Candelaria walk
items.detail: Free museum with Botero's signature voluminous art plus Picasso, Monet, and Dalí. Then wander the cobblestone streets and graffiti of La Candelaria.

items.title: Monserrate sunset
items.detail: Cable car or funicular to 3,152m summit. Panoramic views of the entire city at golden hour. Entry ~COP 23,000 ($6) round trip. Bring a jacket — it's cold at the top.

items.title: Dinner in Zona G
items.detail: Bogotá's gourmet district. Try Colombian-fusion at Leo Cocina y Cava or Criterion. Budget COP 80,000–120,000 ($20–30) for a special meal.

items.title: Flight to Armenia (AXM)
items.detail: Avianca or LATAM, ~55 min. Arrive at El Edén Airport. Then shared van or bus to Salento (~1 hr, COP 10,000).

items.title: Coffee & breakfast on the plaza
items.detail: Fresh Colombian coffee and calentado (reheated beans, rice, egg, and arepa) at one of the colorful cafés on Plaza de Bolívar.

items.title: Coffee finca tour
items.detail: Visit a working coffee farm to see the full bean-to-cup process. Finca El Ocaso or Don Elías are popular choices. ~COP 25,000 ($6) with tasting.

items.title: Lunch — Fresh trout on Calle Real
items.detail: Salento is famous for trucha (trout). Try it grilled with patacones at one of the restaurants along colorful Calle Real. ~COP 20,000 ($5).

items.title: Calle Real & artisan shops
items.detail: Browse handmade jewelry, woven mochilas (bags), and local coffee. The colorful balconied buildings are quintessential Coffee Region.

items.title: Mirador Alto de la Cruz
items.detail: Climb 250+ stairs to the hilltop viewpoint for sweeping views of the Cocora Valley and surrounding green mountains. Best light in late afternoon.

items.title: Check into Salento hostel or finca
items.detail: Stay at a countryside finca for the full coffee region experience, or a hostel in town for easy access to nightlife and restaurants.

items.title: Willys jeep to Cocora Valley
items.detail: Iconic open-back Willys jeeps depart from the plaza when full. 30-min ride through mountain roads. COP 4,000 each way.

items.title: Valle de Cocora hike
items.detail: Full loop trail (~5 hr, 12 km) through cloud forest past the world's tallest palm trees (wax palms, up to 60m). Trail can be muddy — boots recommended. Entry ~COP 5,000.

items.title: Acaime Hummingbird Sanctuary
items.detail: Midway through the loop trail, stop at Acaime for hot chocolate and dozens of hummingbird species buzzing around feeders. Entry COP 7,000 + hot drink.

items.title: Willys jeep back to Salento
items.detail: Catch a return jeep from the valley entrance. They run regularly throughout the day.

items.title: Farewell lunch in Salento
items.detail: Last meal on the plaza — try bandeja paisa or another round of fresh trucha. Pick up some locally roasted coffee beans to take home.

items.title: Bus to Armenia airport
items.detail: Shared van or bus back to El Edén Airport (~1 hr). Allow extra time for mountain roads.

items.title: Depart Armenia (AXM)
items.detail: Evening flight back to Bogotá for connection or onward travel.
